pregame_model_read.pre_bet_thesis: Wicks 4+ was identified as a plus-money threshold play sitting near the model median. Risk flags included short leash, lower raw whiff ceiling, and dependence on early changeup command.

pitch_notes.four_seam_fastball: 15 pitches, 0 K, 27% CSW; strike-heavy but not enough swing-miss.
pitch_notes.changeup: 12 pitches, 1 K, 25% CSW; only direct K source but insufficient volume/effectiveness.
pitch_notes.sinker: 10 pitches, 0 K, 10% CSW; contact-heavy and contributed to early traffic.
pitch_notes.slider: 8 pitches, 0 K, 25% CSW; limited usage and no putaway conversion.

model_evaluation.signal_result: failed due to leash collapse and whiff shortfall
what_failed: Wicks lasted only 2.0 innings, removing the runway needed for a 4+ threshold.
CSW finished at only 22%, well below the 29-31% live green threshold.
Whiff rate was only 16% on swings, confirming the K path was not live.
Despite 65% strike rate and 70% first-pitch strike rate, he did not generate putaway quality.
The plus-money threshold was attractive, but the model underweighted short-leash downside.
model_evaluation.patch_recommendation: Add WICKS_TYPE_SHORT_LEASH_GATE: for low-ceiling/deception lefties with projected mean near the line, require stronger leash confidence before betting median thresholds. Avoid equal stake with stronger structural K profiles.

notes: Wicks 4+ was a price-to-threshold value play that failed badly because the pitcher lasted only two innings and produced just one strikeout. This should be classified as a leash-collapse miss, not a hook burn.
